business.headline: Gold drops over Rs 3,400, silver cheaper by Rs 11,000: Is it time to invest?Despite global tensions, gold and silver turned cheaper, with gold down Rs 3,400 and silver down Rs 11,000. Does this decline signal a good entry point for investors?

Government urges LPG users to switch to PNG as companies roll out incentivesSujata Sharma, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, said the move has been discussed over the past few days as authorities look to ensure stable fuel availability across the country.
Sensex ends 939 points higher, Nifty above 23,300; SBI up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 938.93 points to end at 75,502.85, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 257.70 points to close at 23,408.80.

Saudi Arabia offers alternate Red Sea route as Strait of Hormuz faces crisisSaudi Arabia is now giving its long-term customers the option to receive their April oil allocations through the Red Sea port of Yanbu instead of the usual Persian Gulf terminals.

Citigroup lowers Nifty target to 27,000 amid war risksThe global brokerage has cut its Nifty target to 27,000 from the earlier estimate of 28,500. Even after the reduction, the new target still suggests a possible upside of about 17% from the Nifty's last closing level.

Buying motor insurance? Why the fine print may matter more than the premiumMotor insurance is intended to shift risk from households. Mis-selling this type of insurance has the reverse effect, leaving the consumer vulnerable at the very time they are seeking protection.

PhonePe delays IPO as Iran conflict cools primary marketPhonePe said on Monday that it has temporarily deferred its public market listing due to rising uncertainty in global markets linked to the conflict in West Asia. The company added that it will restart the process once conditions in capital markets become more stable.

Bandhan Bank share price crashes 10%: Why is the stock down today?Bandhan Bank's stock was trading at around Rs 159.50 at about 1.50 pm, down 9.19% on the National Stock Exchange (BSE). Read to know why the shares of the bank tumbled today.

Central Mine Planning IPO: Price set at Rs 172. Worth subscribing as per GMP?The Central Mine Planning IPO is a book-built issue and the entire offer consists of an offer for sale. A total of 10.71 crore shares will be sold in the issue, taking the total size of the IPO to Rs 1,842.12 crore.

IDBI Bank share price crashes 16%: Why is the stock down today?A 16% crash in IDBI Bank shares has caught the market's attention. Such sharp moves rarely happen without a reason, leaving investors asking what exactly went wrong today.

How many pensioners are under OPS and NPS? Govt shares numbersThere has been growing discussion around OPS and NPS in recent years, especially among government employees. The Centre has now revealed the latest numbers of pensioners under both schemes in Parliament. So how many pensioners are covered under OPS and NPS?

Why has the Income Tax Department asked taxpayers to ignore advance tax emails?Many taxpayers were surprised to receive emails about advance tax payments mentioning "significant transactions". But soon after complaints surfaced, the Income Tax Department asked people to ignore those messages. So what exactly went wrong?

Buying the market dip? Why index funds may be the smarter betMarkets will rise and fall, but discipline often decides long-term returns. For investors unsure about timing the next move, index funds offer a simple way to stay invested and ride the market. Here's why they could be worth a closer look.

Sensex jumps 1,000 points despite Iran war. Why is the stock market rising today?The BSE Sensex surged during the session and was trading at 75,802.18 at around 2:55 pm, up 1,238.26 points or 1.66%. The NSE Nifty also moved higher and was trading near 23,493.15, up 342.05 points.
Sensex, Nifty open flat as energy supply crisis still weighs on Dalal StreetThe S&P BSE Sensex was down 98.52 points to 74,465.40, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 28.75 points to 23,122.35 as of 9:27 am.

Highlights: LPG production up, online bookings rise; states act against hoardingLPG crisis Highlights: LPG tanker Shivalik arrived at Gujarat's Mundra Port after safely crossing the conflict-hit Strait of Hormuz, following transit clearance from Iranian authorities. Another Indian-flagged LPG carrier, Nanda Devi, is expected to reach Kandla on March 17 with a similar cargo. Meanwhile, concerns over cooking gas supply are affecting several sectors, with restaurants, hostels and some companies reporting disruptions in kitchens and food services. Top private schools in Delhi have warned that lunch services could be affected if LPG supplies tighten further, while malls in the national capital have asked restaurants to operate conservatively and manage with about 80% gas supply. The government, however, has urged citizens not to panic, saying oil companies are delivering around 50 lakh LPG cylinders every day and supplies remain intact. Authorities have also stepped up vigilance against rumours, hoarding and black marketing of cylinders. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government measures and the impact across cities and industries in India.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Early signals from the derivatives market suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,257 at around 08:11 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open above Friday's closing level of 23,151.10.

upGrad to acquire Unacademy in all-stock deal, Gaurav Munjal to remain CEOThe acquisition signals a major consolidation in India's online education space, bringing together two leading edtech players under a unified, AI-driven growth strategy.

BNW's Smita Nair sees strong demand for UAE luxury homesSpeaking at a session at the India Today Conclave, Nair said demand for ultra-luxury and branded residences remains strong even as parts of the mid-market segment show caution.

Luxury, waterfront homes driving UAE real estate demand: BNW's Smita NairSpeaking at a breakaway session at the India Today Conclave 2026, Smita Nair said demand for ultra-luxury and branded residences remains strong, particularly among international investors.

entertainment.headline: Vir Das on why India rarely has 'Gervais or Conan-style' hosting at film awards Vir Das shared why roast-style hosting like Ricky Gervais or Conan O'Brien at international award shows rarely works in India. He said star egos and industry power dynamics make it difficult for comedians to openly joke about top actors.

Shreya Ghoshal says she rejected Dabangg 2 hit Fevicol Se: Too much objectification Shreya Ghoshal shared that she refused to sing the popular song Fevicol Se from Dabangg 2 because she felt the lyrics were too objectifying.

Actor Jannat Zubair, brother Ayaan safe after alleged assault and highway chase Jannat Zubair and her brother Ayaan Zubair are safe after an alleged physical assault and highway chase incident in Panvel, Mumbai. In a statement, her team confirmed that the police are investigating the matter.

Rapper Raftaar says he has no connection to Rajkummar Rao, Keerthy Suresh's Raftaar Rapper Raftaar clarified that he has no connection to the upcoming film Raftaar starring Rajkummar Rao and Keerthy Suresh. The clarification came after some fans assumed the film might be linked to the rapper.

Ireland celebrates Jessie Buckley as country's first Best Actress Oscar winner Jessie Buckley created history by becoming the first Irish Best Actress Oscar winner. President Catherine Connolly congratulated Buckley on her achievement.

Even Vijju was Team Bride: Rashmika Mandanna, Vijay Deverakonda share Haldi pics Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna shared colourful glimpses from their Haldi ceremony. Rashmika shared that the celebration felt like Haldi + Holi and was filled with games, colours, and family traditions.

Apoorva Lakhia on why Salman Khan's war drama was renamed Maatrubhumi Apoorva Lakhia opened up on the reason behind Salman Khan's Battle of Galwan being renamed Maatrubhumi. He said that the title change was not an "overnight decision."

Viral: Michael B Jordan hugs Leonardo DiCaprio after winning Best Actor Michael B Jordan won his first Best Actor Oscar for his film Sinners at the 98th Academy Awards. After the win, fellow nominee Leonardo DiCaprio congratulated him, and the two shared a warm hug.

Hema Malini calls Dharmendra's omission from Oscars In Memoriam a 'shame' Hema Malini expressed disappointment after Dharmendra was left out of the televised In Memoriam segment at the Oscars. Calling the omission a "shame," she said the late actor was loved by audiences across the world.

Kanye West's New Delhi concert gets a new date amid global tensions Kanye West's highly anticipated concert in New Delhi on March 29 has been postponed due to rising global tensions. Organisers have assured fans that all tickets remain valid for the new date.

Armaan Malik slams Nora Fatehi's Sarke Chunar Teri Sarke lyrics Nora Fatehi's song Sarke Chunar Teri Sarke from KD: The Devil has sparked backlash online over its lyrics. Armaan Malik called the track "a new low".

Janhvi Kapoor pens note after celebrity hairstylist Marce Pedrozo's death Janhvi Kapoor shared an emotional tribute to hairstylist Marce Pedrozo after his death, recalling their close working relationship and memories from film sets.

Nawazuddin Siddiqui says he's not here to fill vacuum left by Irrfan Nawazuddin Siddiqui said he does not see himself as filling the void left by late actor Irrfan. He stressed that his journey in cinema is about personal growth and self-discovery.

Meet Alexandre Singh, Indian-origin filmmaker who won at Oscars 2026 Indian-origin writer-filmmaker Alexandre Singh is the co-director of Two People Exchanging Saliva, which won the 2026 Oscar for Best Live Action Short Film.  Here's all there's to know about the talented filmmaker.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan revising committee screening tomorrow: Sources The revising committee of the Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC) is expected to watch Vijay's Jana Nayagan on March 17.  The film was supposed to release in January 2026, but was postponed due to non-issuance of a censor certificate.

Shreya Ghoshal backs Arijit Singh's decision: I also feel like taking a break Singer Shreya Ghoshal praised Arijit Singh for his "brave" decision to step away from playback singing. She also admitted that the thought of taking a break from playback singing has crossed her mind at times.

Khushbu Sundar backs Trisha over public appearance with Vijay. Read what she said Actor-politician Khushbu Sundar has spoken out against the social media trolling actor Trisha is facing after attending a wedding with Vijay. She praised Trisha's dignity and condemned the growing paparazzi culture in Chennai.

Michael B Jordan celebrates Best Actor Oscar win with a burger at fast food outlet Michael B Jordan celebrated his Oscar win as he stopped at Hollywood In-N-Out after the awards ceremony. The actor was seen smiling while holding his Best Actor trophy.

Nukkad Naatak team on OTT struggles, limited budget and meaningful cinema Faced with limited options for digital release, Nukkad Naatak released in theatres on February 27. In an exclusive interview, director Tanmaya Shekhar and actor Molshri shared the challenges of small-scale filmmaking and the importance of telling nuanced social stories.

general news.headline: They're talking to our people: Trump says Iran wants a deal to end warSpeaking at the White House as the US bombing campaign against Iran entered its third week, he said Iranian officials had been reaching out to US representatives but suggested that it was unclear whether Tehran was ready to agree to a deal.

More than 200 dead as Afghanistan says Pakistan bombed Kabul rehab hospitalTensions between Afghanistan and Pakistan escalated after Kabul said airstrikes destroyed a drug treatment hospital, killing over 200 people, while Islamabad insisted its operation targeted militant infrastructure.

Biased: India slams 'selective targeting' as US body seeks sanctions on RAW, RSSResponding to media queries, Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said the government "categorically rejects" the report and its characterisation of India. He said the commission has repeatedly presented a "distorted and selective picture" of the country.

NDA wins all 5 Rajya Sabha seats in Bihar, 2 in Odisha; Congress cries foul  Live UpdatesThe NDA won all five Rajya Sabha seats from Bihar after four Opposition MLAs skipped voting, dealing a setback to the Mahagathbandhan. The alliance also secured two seats in Odisha amid cross-voting by Opposition MLAs during the biennial polls.

You Indian? Get lost: Are Indians treated badly in Vietnam?Vietnam has grown popular among Indian travellers, but some say they faced hostility in shops and markets. Tourists and locals suggest stereotypes about Indians may be contributing to the friction.

Saudi Crown Prince asked Trump to keep hitting Iranians hard: NYTThe newspaper, citing officials familiar with the conversations, said the Saudi leader has been in frequent contact with Trump during the conflict and encouraged him to "keep hitting the Iranians hard" as the fighting intensifies across the region.

India says no talks with US on warship deployment in Hormuz after Trump's appealIndia on Monday said it has not held bilateral talks with the United States on deploying naval vessels to secure merchant shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The clarification came after US President Donald Trump urged countries to send warships to keep the strategic waterway open amid tensions with Iran.

Live: About 200 US troops wounded since the start Iran war, military saysUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: In yet another significant escalation in the Middle East, a drone struck near Dubai International Airport, causing a blaze that was eventually contained but left flight operations temporarily suspended. The blaze was caused by the projectile hitting a fuel tank in the vicinity of the major airport. In another development, US President Donald Trump has said that he is in talks with about seven countries to form a coalition to escort merchant ships through the Strait of Hormuz unharmed. He, however, declined to name the countries. But he said he won't forget the countries that decline to help. Coming to the financial toll of the growing conflict, the price of US crude oil has gone above USD 100 per barrel. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.

Is oral sex replacing smoking as the leading cause of throat cancer?While smoking remains the leading cause of throat cancer in India, HPV infections transmitted through oral sex have overtaken tobacco as the primary cause in countries such as the US and the UK.

Netanyahu shares new video after Grok calls out deepfake amid death rumoursDeath rumours about Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu surfaced online after claims he was killed in an Iranian attack, prompting him to release videos -- one of which Grok flagged as AI-generated.

Iran launches Sejjil dancing missile against US: What is it?The missile can carry a payload of around 500-1,000 kilograms, which may include conventional high-explosive warheads and potentially other types depending on configuration.

Buying Pak players contributes to death of Indian soldiers: GavaskarSunil Gavaskar has criticised SunRisers Leeds owners for signing Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed at The Hundred 2026 auction, arguing that Indian owners must not contribute to Pakistani government revenues.

Was Gautam Gambhir behind Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma's Test retirements?Was Gautam Gambhir behind the Test retirements of Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma? India head coach Gautam Gambhir has hinted at the possible reason while speaking in an event in Kolkata.

Suvendu Adhikari vs Mamata Banerjee 2.0? BJP unveils first Bengal listThe BJP has fielded Suvendu Adhikari from both Bhabanipur and Nandigram in its first list for the West Bengal Assembly polls, setting up a direct contest against Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee's stronghold.

US forces obliterated Iran, its air force and navy completely destroyed: TrumpSpeaking ahead of a lunch event at the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts in Washington, Trump said the strikes have largely targeted commercial and military facilities. He added that U.S. forces carried out attacks on three missile and drone manufacturing sites earlier in the day.

A sombre Laylatul Qadr in war-hit Middle East this yearLaylatul Qadr is considered one of the holiest nights in Islam. The Night of Decree, when Muslims keep up late into the night for prayers, is different this year. It is a sombre Laylatul Qadr night amid fears for life and safety amid the US-Israel-Iran war in the Middle East.
Indian fashion shines bright at Oscars 2026Several creations by Indian designers made their way to the Oscars red carpet and the after-party this year.

LPG tanker Shivalik reaches Gujarat port after crossing war-hit Strait of HormuzLPG tanker Shivalik arrived at Gujarat's Mundra Port after safely crossing the conflict-hit Strait of Hormuz, following transit clearance from Iranian authorities. Another Indian-flagged LPG carrier, Nanda Devi, is expected to reach Kandla on March 17 with a similar cargo.

Actor Jannat Zubair, brother Ayaan safe after alleged assault and highway chaseJannat Zubair and her brother Ayaan Zubair are safe after an alleged physical assault and highway chase incident in Panvel, Mumbai. In a statement, her team confirmed that the police are investigating the matter.

Profiles of top Chinese nuclear and missile experts vanish. Another Xi purge?The profiles of at least three of China's leading nuclear, missile and radar experts were scrubbed from the website of the Chinese Academy of Engineering, the country's most prestigious academic body. This comes as a series of purges under Premier Xi Jinping's anti-corruption campaign have decimated the upper echelons of China's military and scientific community.

Ahura and Asura: Iran and India are brothers linked by Gods and sacred fireHandcrafted ByOur Editors When we look at Iran through the prism of religion and see a Shia Islamic country, we negate its thousands of years of rich pre-Islamic Persian culture. A dive into the world of Zoroastrianism and Vedas shows us how Indians and Iranians have been sharing languages, Gods, sciences and a sacred fire for thousands of years.

OpenAI delays Adult mode release after suicide cases rise due to ChatGPTOpenAI has reportedly postponed the rollout of ChatGPT's proposed adult mode amid internal concerns and rising scrutiny over the chatbot's role in sensitive conversations. The delay comes as the company faces lawsuits and criticism following suicide cases linked to extended interactions with the AI.

How desperate Trump wants others to be sucked into Hormuz hurricaneWashington DC declared victory within a day of the strikes on Iran. Three weeks later, the Strait of Hormuz remains the most volatile it has ever been. President Trump now wants other countries to help end the Hormuz stalemate with military support. However, there are few takers of his plan.

Bengaluru cafe charges customer 5% 'gas crisis' fee on lemonade bill amid LPG crisisIn a post on X, a user shared a picture of the receipt from Bengaluru's Theo Cafe, which shows a 5 per cent "gas crisis charge" of Rs 17.01 applied to an order of two mint lemonades priced at Rs 179 each. The beverages together cost Rs 358.

sports.headline: KKR's fast bowling problem: 2024 winners head towards shaky start to IPL campaign Kolkata Knight Riders enter the new IPL season with a fast-bowling unit already battered by injuries and uncertainty. With Harshit Rana unlikely to feature and Matheesha Pathirana still recovering, the defending champions suddenly look short of pace options and experience.

Pratika Rawal opens up on her deep bond with Test cricket: This is my format Pratika Rawal said that she considers Test cricket the ultimate measure of a cricketer's character, patience, and determination. She made her debut in the Day-Night Perth Test against Australia, scoring her maiden fifty.

Chennai Super Kings appoint ex-England wicketkeeper as fielding coach for IPL 2026 Chennai Super Kings have appointed former England wicketkeeper-batter James Foster as their new fielding coach ahead of IPL 2026, adding an experienced coach to Stephen Fleming's support staff.

Liam Livingstone slams Brendon McCullum, Rob Key in sensational rant against ECB Liam Livingstone launched a blistering attack on England's team management, accusing Brendon McCullum and Rob Key of poor communication and a lack of support after being dropped from the white-ball setup.

The story of how Suryakumar, not Hardik Pandya, became India's T20I captain Suryakumar Yadav was made the T20I captain after the 2024 World Cup, pipping the then vice-captain Hardik Pandya. The batter reveals how he got to know about the news.

Vaishnavi Adkar continues dream run, closes in on India's No.1 WTA ranking Vaishnavi Adkar's rapid rise continued as she broke into the WTA top 400 for the first time, climbing to a career-best No. 397 after winning the ITF W35 Kalaburagi title and moving within six spots of India's No.1 ranking.

Pakistan fast bowler grabs limelight with massive no-ball in National T20 Cup Pakistan pacer Asad Akhtar grabbed attention after bowling a huge no-ball during Karachi Blues' National T20 Cup clash against Lahore Blues on Saturday at the Imran Khan Cricket Stadium in Peshawar.

Chelsea fined 131 crore rupees, 1-year-ban for breaching financial rules Chelsea have been fined $14.3 million and handed a suspended transfer ban after the Premier League found the club guilty of breaching financial regulations. Undisclosed payments, made between 2011 and 2018 have landed the club in trouble.

It is not Shubman Gill: Fans slam BCCI over huge mistake in Naman Awards The Indian Cricket Board is being called out for an extremely silly mistake at the Naman Awards 2026. During the ceremony, BCCI credited Shubman Gill as the captain who won the Champions Trophy, and not Rohit Sharma.

Antonelli arrives, Hamilton makes comeback in scintillating Shanghai showdown Shanghai GP: Kimi Antonelli's maiden win, Lewis Hamilton's Ferrari debut podium, and Max Verstappen's frustration headlined a thrilling Chinese Grand Prix as Formula 1's 2026 season bursts into life in Shanghai.

MS Dhoni always has a positive influence on me: Sanju Samson eager for CSK stint Sanju Samson said he is excited to share the CSK dressing room with MS Dhoni in IPL 2026, adding that the former India captain has always had a positive influence on him and will be a valuable source of learning.

Unleash the real Hardik Pandya: Harbhajan Singh on what MI needs to win IPL 2026IPL 2026: Harbhajan Singh believes that the Mumbai Indians need to find a way to bring out the best in Hardik Pandya should they stand a chance of getting back to winning titles.

Ricky Ponting explains the bold call that shaped India's T20 WC title defenceRicky Ponting said India's bold call to restore Sanju Samson as opener and move Ishan Kishan to No. 3 played a major role in their successful T20 World Cup 2026 title defence.

Fastest IPL hundred or Chris Gayle's 175? Vaibhav Sooryavanshi makes his pickDuring the Naman Awards on Sunday, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi revealed that he aspires to break Chris Gayle's long-standing record of 175, the highest individual score in T20 cricket.

Buying Pak players contributes to death of Indian soldiers: GavaskarSunil Gavaskar has criticised SunRisers Leeds owners for signing Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed at The Hundred 2026 auction, arguing that Indian owners must not contribute to Pakistani government revenues.

No India T20 World Cup cricketer nominated for February's Player of the Month awardDespite India's successful T20 World Cup title defence, no Indian men's cricketer was shortlisted for the ICC Men's Player of the Month award for February. But India have representation in the women's category, with pacer Arundhati Reddy nominated.

India can field two playing XIs: Suryakumar Yadav hails country's talent poolIndia captain Suryakumar Yadav believes the country's T20 talent pool has grown so deep that multiple international-quality teams could be formed. He credits domestic cricket and the IPL for building India's remarkable strength in the format.

Was Gautam Gambhir behind Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma's Test retirements?Was Gautam Gambhir behind the Test retirements of Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma? India head coach Gautam Gambhir has hinted at the possible reason while speaking in an event in Kolkata.

India dominating cricket on all fronts: Mithali Raj calls it a dream come trueFormer India captain Mithali Raj believes Indian cricket is finally living up to its long-held potential. With the men's, women's and junior teams winning global titles, she says the country is witnessing a proud era of dominance.

Karnataka government clears Chinnaswamy stadium to host IPL 2026 matchesIPL 2026: Karnataka government has approved IPL matches at Chinnaswamy Stadium after an expert committee reviewed safety and preparedness measures at a meeting chaired by Home Minister G. Parameshwara.

Who is Max Dowman? Arsenal's wonderkid now Premier League's youngest scorerArsenal teenager Max Dowman made Premier League history with a stunning goal against Everton. At just 16 years and 73 days, the winger became the youngest goalscorer the league has ever seen.

Can Carrick bring the good times back to Manchester United? Rooney shares his stanceSeven wins from nine games have taken Manchester United from sixth to third under Michael Carrick, with Wayne Rooney and fans growing louder in their calls for him to be handed the job permanently.

Tried to make Gambhir laugh many times during T20 World Cup, reveals Suryakumar YadavSuryakumar Yadav revealed India's players tried repeatedly to make coach Gautam Gambhir laugh during the T20 World Cup 2026, finally succeeding after their title win in Ahmedabad on Sunday night.

Ahmedabad curse behind him, Suryakumar wishes he could replay 2023 World Cup finalSuryakumar Yadav says lifting the T20 World Cup 2026 trophy in Ahmedabad has finally put to rest the pain of the 2023 ODI World Cup final defeat at the same venue, the memory that haunted him most.

Watch: KKR injury worries grow as Harshit Rana spotted on crutches, IPL 2026 in doubtKKR's preparations for IPL 2026 could suffer a major blow as fast bowler Harshit Rana's injury concerns grow. The young pacer was recently seen on crutches, raising doubts over his availability for the upcoming season.

Ex-India coach Rahul Dravid proud of Sanju Samson's comeback journey in T20 World CupRahul Dravid has praised Sanju Samson's remarkable comeback during India's triumphant 2026 T20 World Cup campaign. The wicketkeeper-batter delivered decisive performances in the knockout stages and emerged as Player of the Tournament.

India winning the 2027 ODI World Cup is the ultimate goal: Shubman GillIndia captain Shubman Gill has set his sights on the 2027 ODI World Cup, calling it the ultimate target for the team after their heartbreak in the 2023 final. The skipper believes India have another shot at glory.

No Messi vs Yamal as UEFA cancels Spain vs Argentina Finalissima over Middle East warFootball fans will have to wait longer for the dream clash between Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal. The 2026 Finalissima between Spain and Argentina has been cancelled due to escalating tensions in the Middle East.

Sinner outlasts Medvedev in gritty Indian Wells final, joins hard-court elitesJannik Sinner edged Daniil Medvedev in a tense Indian Wells final to complete a rare hard-court Masters sweep. The Italian joined Novak Djokovic and Roger Federer as the only men to win all six ATP Masters 1000 hard-court titles.

Pakistan slump further after T20 World Cup debacle, lose ODI series to BangladeshBAN vs PAK, 3rd ODI: Bangladesh beat Pakistan by 11 runs in the third and final ODI on Sunday at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium to win the series 2-1.

Samson reveals Sooryavanshi's carefree chat with Dravid: Pehla hi uda dengeDuring the Naman Awards, Sanju Samson revealed that during IPL 2025, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi confidently told Rahul Dravid he would simply play his natural game, even planning to go aerial on the first opportunity.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
